div.head: VISIT REPORT
ICL Putney
ICL CUA CAFS SIG WP
14 Feb 1985
div.p: This was the first meeting between members of the SIG working party and the "panel" of ICL heavies carefully chosen by the appalling D. Lovegrove to field the working party's assorted googlies and bouncers. Heavies actually present in addition to said Lovegrove were the customary Carmichael, the ubiquitous Haworth, the really obnoxious J. Finch (putting in a late appearance between really important meetings elsewhere as usual) and two human beings from Kidsgrove called Barry Hopewell and Jim Lucking.

After some elaborate foreplay in which Lovegrove attempted to take over the chair, the meeting settled down to going through the more recent of the wp's two wishlists, explicating where necessary (often) and categorising each item as A (ICL would do) B (it wouldnt) or C (for later discussion). A revised and homogenised list of items tagged C will be circulated for the next meeting, by when ICL should have responded on most of its content. Most of my tricky bits of text handling were categorised as "well known Bs", to no one's particular surprise.

After lunch, work on the Report continued. The apportioning of sections, and what was expected in each, were firmed up and drafts were promised for the next meeting. Hearing that money was available for the purpose, I offered to get a quote for lasercomping the report at OUCS.

I also attempted to determine the ICL view on the IDM (Britton Lee's competing hardware database) and learned that it is not selling better than CAFS (but not much worse either); my suggestion that it would be interesting to run the Britton Lee benchmark on a CAFS system was not rejected out of hand.

Other minor points of interest noted during the day: the SV211 VME "Package Designers" (?) manual incorporates much concerning CAFS design aids... ICL Service Desks will be CAFS-ized eventually... CAFS support for DDS is probable... QM250 spec is still under review and now delayed until the summer... The announcement concerning new products (you know, Estriels, discs that fly etc) is still pending, but when it's made I'll be able to tell you that at least one controller on any installation will have a CAFS board at no extra charge.
